root_motivation: The bi-toolkit vision needs a deterministic, pure-Rust step that maps a typed column inventory (result-profile.v1) to a chart mark and encoding, so downstream emitters (Vega) receive a schema-valid recommendation instead of requiring the LLM to re-inspect data or guess chart shapes each time.
user_persona: AtScale MCP server or LLM orchestrator calling the lib as part of the bi-toolkit pipeline: produces chart-recommendation.v1 JSON from result-profile.v1 input, no render dependency, no network.

acceptance_criteria.description: A profile with 1 measure + 1 temporal dim → mark = Line, x = temporal field, y = measure field.
A profile with 1 measure + 1 nominal dim (low cardinality) → mark = Bar.
A profile with 2 measures, 0 dims → mark = Point (scatter), x and y being the two measures.
A profile with 1 measure, 0 dims → mark = BigNumber.
A high-cardinality nominal dim (> 25) still returns mark = Bar but alternatives contains Table with a cardinality rationale.
A 0-measure profile → mark = Table with a rationale explaining nothing is quantitative.
Every recommendation carries a non-empty rationale and deterministic alternatives ordering.

scope: Accept result-profile.v1 JSON (serde_json::Value or local mirror struct)
Implement the full selection rule table from the PRD (BigNumber, Bar, Line, Point, Rect, Table marks)
Emit chart-recommendation.v1 JSON: {mark, encoding, rationale, alternatives}
non_goals: Rendering or Vega-Lite spec emission (that is mqo-vega-emitter)
Reading rows or the catalog directly — only result-profile.v1 consumed
theta/pie encodings beyond leaving the channel None in v1
Integration with mqo-result-profiler as a Rust crate dependency

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the bi-toolkit need a chart recommender at all?
five_whys_trace.a: The LLM downstream receives a result profile but has no deterministic step to choose a chart — it guesses, producing inconsistent results.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't the LLM just choose the chart each time from the data?
five_whys_trace.a: The profile already encodes role, temporality, and cardinality — the information needed for the choice is fully present; re-inspecting data wastes tokens and introduces variance.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a pure Rust lib rather than a prompt template?
five_whys_trace.a: A prompt template is not testable, not versioned as code, and cannot gate on failing acceptance tests; a Rust lib can be unit-tested, deployed as a binary, and embedded in the MCP server.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does the lib need mqo-result-profiler as a Rust crate dep?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No — it reads result-profile.v1 JSON (serde_json::Value or a mirrored local struct). No crate dependency on mqo-result-profiler.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is theta/pie encoding in scope for v1?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Non-goal: leave theta channel as None in v1.
